ALL SPECIALTIES / DIETITIAN / COLORADO

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Colorado Dietitian Registration

Colorado Dietitian license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dietitian applications in Colorado run through State Board of Dietetics and Nutrition (New). Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • CDR Registration Verification - Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • CBI Background Check Fingerprints - Colorado Bureau of Investigation background check, processed at a DORA-approved provider.
  • Proof of Supervised Practice - Verification of at least 1,000 hours of supervised practice from an ACEND-accredited program.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing N/A — No State License Required
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Details on reciprocity or endorsement will be established by the new Board of Dietetics and Nutrition under DORA regulations. Active CDR registration will serve as the primary qualification for state licensure.

  1. STEP 1

    No State Licensure Required

    The State of Colorado does not regulate dietetics or nutrition services. Therefore, there are no state-mandated steps or an application process to practice as a dietitian or nutritionist within Colorado.

    Fee: ~$239.50 (Estimated) · Timeline: N/A — No State License Required

  2. STEP 2

    Professional Certification (Optional)

    While not required by the state, many professionals pursue national certifications such as the Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) credential from the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) to demonstrate competency and enhance professional standing.

    Fee: ~$239.50 (Estimated) · Timeline: N/A — No State License Required

  3. STEP 3

    Employer Requirements

    Employers in Colorado may still require specific qualifications, certifications (like RDN), education, and experience for dietitian and nutritionist positions, even without state licensure.

    Fee: ~$239.50 (Estimated) · Timeline: N/A — No State License Required

  4. STEP 4

    Local Regulations (Check with Municipality)

    It is advisable to check with local county or city governments for any specific local business licenses or regulations that might apply, although state-level professional licensure is not applicable.

    Fee: ~$239.50 (Estimated) · Timeline: N/A — No State License Required

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$200
Background check
$39.50
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
~$239.50 (Estimated)

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
State Board of Dietetics and Nutrition (New)
Phone
(303) 894-7800
Website
dpo.colorado.gov/
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Dietitian renewal in Colorado follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Annual (Estimated) + 75 CPE hours per 5-year CDR cycle
Renewal fee
~$125–$180 (Estimated)
Continuing education
75 continuing professional education (CPE) hours per 5-year CDR cycle

Frequently Asked Questions

When does Colorado's dietitian license requirement take effect?

The new licensure requirement takes effect on September 1, 2026. After this date, no person may practice or offer medical nutrition therapy without a state license.

How much will a Colorado dietitian license cost?

The estimated initial registration fee is approximately $200 for FY 2026-27, plus a $39.50 CBI background check. Annual renewal is estimated between $125 and $180.

Do I need a license before September 1, 2026?

No. Before September 1, 2026, Colorado does not mandate state licensure. The CDR credential (RD/RDN) remains the recognized standard until the new law takes effect.

Does Colorado have reciprocity for dietitians?

Colorado is currently a CDR State. There is no separate state endorsement transfer pathway right now; maintain an active CDR credential to practice.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Colorado?

This is currently a CDR State, so there is no state transfer timeline. You can usually start once your CDR credential and employer onboarding are complete.

Can I start working in Colorado while my endorsement application is processing?

There is no active state endorsement application to wait on right now. Start dates are typically driven by employer credentialing and onboarding requirements.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

~$239.50 (Estimated)

Processing time

N/A — No State License Required

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

Registration Examination for Dietitians

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Annual (Estimated) + 75 CPE hours per 5-year CDR cycle · ~$125–$180 (Estimated)

Licensing board
